compressed_prompt: write song lyrics on socio-economic issue; english, all lowercase; no commas
response:
**"concrete jungle"**

i wake up to the hum of the city lights
another day another dollar tight
the rent’s due but the paycheck’s thin
just enough to keep me trapped within

the bus ride’s long the seats are cold
the driver’s tired the story’s old
same old faces same old pain
same old promises blown away like rain

they say pull yourself up by your bootstraps
but my boots got holes and my straps are loose
they say work hard play by the rules
but the game’s rigged and the deck’s not new

the skyline’s tall but the cracks run deep
where the dreams go to die in their sleep
another factory closes another job lost
another family frozen in the frost

they talk about growth they talk about change
but the numbers don’t match the names
the rich get richer the poor get poor
the same old cycle we can’t ignore

so i walk these streets with my head held low
another ghost in the afterglow
another voice in the endless crowd
another soul screaming but nobody’s loud

the system’s broken but we keep it running
like a car with no gas just keep on gunning
another day another dollar tight
another night in the concrete jungle’s light
